text.title: Feature Flags Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying feature-flags. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Codebase has 47 flag evaluations; nobody knows what 23 of them do.
antipattern.root-cause: No owner + no expiry policy enforced.
antipattern.fix: Add an audit script that flags entries without owner/expiry. Require an ADR-justified removal or refresh for each orphan.

antipattern.symptom: Flag evaluation writes an analytics event synchronously; under load, the write path saturates and slows every request.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wanted exposure tracking; embedded it in the evaluation call.
antipattern.fix: Move side effects (analytics writes) into an async exposure pipeline; keep evaluation pure. Add latency budget guard on evaluation path.

antipattern.symptom: Flag rolled to 100% six months ago; eval branches still pollute the code.
antipattern.fix: Enforce cleanup_pr_url on flag creation. Auto-merge ready-state cleanup PRs once flag is at 100% for >=14 days.

antipattern.symptom: Code path has 4 nested flag branches; nobody can reason about which combination ships.
antipattern.root-cause: Each feature added its own flag without rationalising with neighbours.
antipattern.fix: Refactor to a single composite flag with explicit variants. Add lint rule for nesting depth >2.
